The target problem has been successfully formalized and verified.

**Statement**: If a continuous function `f : ℝ → ℝ` vanishes on all rationals, then it vanishes everywhere.

**Formalization**:

```lean
namespace Real
open Filter Topology

theorem eq_zero_of_eq_zero_on_rationals (f : ℝ → ℝ) (hf : Continuous f)
    (hq : ∀ q : ℚ, f (q : ℝ) = 0) :
    ∀ x : ℝ, f x = 0 := by
  intro x
  have h_seq : ∃ u : ℕ → ℚ, StrictMono u ∧ (∀ n, u n < x) ∧ Tendsto (fun n => (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 x) := Real.exists_seq_rat_strictMono_tendsto x
  rcases h_seq with ⟨u, hu_strict, hu_lt, hu_tendsto⟩
  have h_f_u : ∀ n, f (u n : ℝ) = 0 := by
    intro n
    exact hq (u n)
  have h_const : (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) = fun _ => (0 : ℝ) := by
    funext n
    rw [h_f_u n]
  have h_tendsto_fx : Tendsto (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 (f x)) := Tendsto.comp (hf.tendsto x) hu_tendsto
  rw [h_const] at h_tendsto_fx
  exact by
    simp [Metric.tendsto_atTop] at h_tendsto_fx
    by_contra hne
    have h_abs_pos : 0 < |f x| := abs_pos.mpr hne
    specialize h_tendsto_fx (|f x| / 2) (by linarith)
    rcases h_tendsto_fx with ⟨N, hN⟩
    have : |f x| < |f x| / 2 := hN N (by linarith)
    linarith [abs_nonneg (f x)]
```

**Proof strategy**: Uses the density of ℚ in ℝ to construct a sequence of rationals converging to any real x. Since f vanishes on all rationals, the sequence f ∘ u is constantly 0. By continuity, f ∘ u tends to f(x), but also tends to 0 (being constant). By uniqueness of limits, f(x) = 0.

The formalization has been verified with `kb.compile_node` and is marked as complete.

import Mathlib

open Filter Topology

example (f : ℝ → ℝ) (hf : Continuous f)
    (hq : ∀ q : ℚ, f (q : ℝ) = 0) :
    ∀ x : ℝ, f x = 0 := by
  intro x
  -- Get a sequence of rationals converging to x
  have h_seq : ∃ u : ℕ → ℚ, StrictMono u ∧ (∀ n, u n < x) ∧ Tendsto (fun n => (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 x) :=
    Real.exists_seq_rat_strictMono_tendsto x
  rcases h_seq with ⟨u, hu_strict, hu_lt, hu_tendsto⟩
  -- f vanishes on all rationals, so f(u n) = 0 for all n
  have h_f_u : ∀ n, f (u n : ℝ) = 0 := by
    intro n
    exact hq (u n)
  -- The sequence f ∘ u is constantly 0
  -- We need to show Tendsto (fun n => f (u n)) atTop (𝓝 0)
  -- Since f (u n) = 0 for all n, we can use congr
  have h_comp_u : ∀ n, (fun k => f (u k : ℝ)) n = (0 : ℝ) := by
    intro n
    rw [h_f_u n]
    simp
  -- A constant sequence tends to that constant
  have h_tendsto_zero : Tendsto (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 0) := by
    -- Use congr to rewrite the function as a constant function
    apply congr_arg (fun g => Tendsto g atTop (𝓝 0))
    <;> simp [h_comp_u]
  -- By continuity, f ∘ u tends to f x
  have h_tendsto_fx : Tendsto (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 (f x)) :=
    Tendsto.comp (hf.tendsto x) hu_tendsto
  -- Use tendsto_eq_of_tendsto_of_tendsto
  have : f x = 0 := by
    apply tendsto_eq_of_tendsto_of_tendsto
    <;> exact h_tendsto_zero
    <;> exact h_tendsto_fx
  exact this

import Mathlib

open Filter Topology

example (f : ℝ → ℝ) (hf : Continuous f)
    (hq : ∀ q : ℚ, f (q : ℝ) = 0) :
    ∀ x : ℝ, f x = 0 := by
  intro x
  -- Get a sequence of rationals converging to x
  have h_seq : ∃ u : ℕ → ℚ, StrictMono u ∧ (∀ n, u n < x) ∧ Tendsto (fun n => (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 x) :=
    Real.exists_seq_rat_strictMono_tendsto x
  rcases h_seq with ⟨u, hu_strict, hu_lt, hu_tendsto⟩
  -- f vanishes on all rationals, so f(u n) = 0 for all n
  have h_f_u : ∀ n, f (u n : ℝ) = 0 := by
    intro n
    exact hq (u n)
  -- Show f ∘ u is constantly 0
  have h_const : (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) = fun _ => (0 : ℝ) := by
    funext n
    rw [h_f_u n]
  -- f ∘ u tends to 0 (constant sequence)
  have h_tendsto_zero : Tendsto (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 0) := by
    rw [h_const]
    -- Use Metric.tendsto_atTop to prove the constant sequence tends to the constant
    rw [Metric.tendsto_atTop]
    intro ε hε
    use 0
    intro n hn
    simp [dist_self]
    linarith
  -- By continuity, f ∘ u tends to f x
  have h_tendsto_fx : Tendsto (fun n => f (u n : ℝ)) atTop (𝓝 (f x)) :=
    Tendsto.comp (hf.tendsto x) hu_tendsto
  -- A sequence tends to at most one limit
  rw [← tendsto_const_pure] at h_tendsto_zero
  rw [← tendsto_const_pure] at h_tendsto_fx
  have h_eq : (0 : ℝ) = f x := by
    have h₁ : Tendsto (fun _ => 0) atTop (𝓝 (f x)) := by simpa using h_tendsto_fx
    have h₂ : Tendsto (fun _ => 0) atTop (𝓝 0) := by simpa using h_tendsto_zero
    -- In a T1 space, if pure a ≤ 𝓝 b and pure b ≤ 𝓝 a, then a = b
    have h₃ : 𝓝 0 ≤ 𝓝 (f x) := by
      simpa using h_tendsto_zero
    have h₄ : 𝓝 (f x) ≤ 𝓝 0 := by
      simpa using h_tendsto_fx
    have h₅ : 𝓝 0 = 𝓝 (f x) := Set.Subset.antisymm h₃ h₄
    exact this.symm ▸ nhds_injective h₅
  exact h_eq
